negative_variants.description: Laplace Zd Zero Den Filter: keeps the file compilable but forces the observable output path low, so it can pass include/instance smoke checks while failing the required behavior to Apply the zero/denominator transfer function to the input waveform.

Laplace Zd Zero Den Filter: keeps the main operator syntax but collapses the computed value through the wrong condition, so coarse smoke cases may run while boundary and waveform checks for Use laplace_zd() for zero/denominator transfer modeling fail.
Laplace Zd Zero Den Filter: introduces incorrect initialization or state bias, so startup and reset-sensitive checks fail while the model still compiles and produces activity.

Laplace Zd Zero Den Filter: offsets the secondary metric or observable path, so the primary waveform may look plausible while calibrated checker evidence for Apply the zero/denominator transfer function to the input waveform fails.

Laplace Zd Zero Den Filter: scales the final observable response, so compile and rough activity checks can pass while calibrated output checks for Use laplace_zd() for zero/denominator transfer modeling fail.
